commit_scope: Remove the BMF semantic matcher native from sibling kernels and prove BMF object rules execute through the Form runtime.

cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/json.fk form-stdlib/cache.fk form-stdlib/form-ontology-loader.fk form-stdlib/engine.fk form-stdlib/compiler.fk form-stdlib/source-compiler.fk form-stdlib/grammars/bml.fk form-stdlib/tests/bml-generic-source-scanner-boundary-proof.fk
local_validation.notes: The native boundary audit reports no BML/BMF semantic natives in Go/Rust/TypeScript and still requires source_scan_file. The new BMF Form object runtime proof returns 2047 across sibling kernels, covering object, sequence, capture, choice, star, opt, cut, stop, match source spans, and reverse emission through engine.fk. Existing BMF component/source runtime proofs remain green at 1023 and the BML file forward/backward proof remains green at 131071.

ci_validation.notes: Pending push, PR, and GitHub checks.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: BMF object-rule execution now has one semantic owner in Form/runtime code; sibling kernels no longer expose bmf_apply_rule_native.

phase_gate.reason: This closes the host-native BMF matcher leak. Full BML thesis parity remains open: whole companion-corpus compilation, full statement/expression lowering, and complete source-originated execution still need further implementation and proof.

evidence_refs: form/form-kernel-go/main.go, form/form-kernel-rust/src/main.rs, and form/form-kernel-ts/src/kernel.ts no longer register bmf_apply_rule_native.
form/form-stdlib/tests/bmf-form-object-runtime-band.fk proves BMF object-rule primitives through engine.fk across sibling kernels.
scripts/audit_bml_bmf_kernel_natives.py now allows no BML/BMF semantic natives and fails if bml_scan_file or bmf_apply_rule_native re-enter kernels.
